items.front: When may a radar approach be offered? (5-10-1b)
items.back: To any aircraft upon request; may be offered to aircraft in distress regardless of weather, or to expedite traffic. Acceptance does NOT waive weather minima - pilot is responsible for determining authorization.

items.front: Approach information items for radar approach (5-10-2a)
items.back: (1) Altimeter setting. (2) Ceiling/visibility if ceiling <1,000 ft or <highest circling minimum, or visibility <3 miles. (3) Special weather observations asap. (4) Pertinent airport condition info. (5) Lost comm procedures per 5-10-4. Transmissions ~every minute. Most items can be omitted for repeated approaches if no change.

items.front: Before starting final approach - what to issue (5-10-2b)
items.back: (1) Type of approach, runway, airport/heliport. Specify airport name for secondary airports. (2) For surveillance approaches: MAP location in relation to runway/airport/heliport.
Inform aircraft: 'NO TRAFFIC OR LANDING RUNWAY INFORMATION AVAILABLE FOR THE AIRPORT.'

(a) Before vector: inform aircraft of approach type - 'THIS WILL BE A NO-GYRO SURVEILLANCE/PAR APPROACH.' (b) Controller issues TURN LEFT/RIGHT and STOP TURN commands. (c) After turn-on to final, prior to approach gate: instruct 'MAKE HALF-STANDARD RATE TURNS.'

items.front: Lost comm time intervals for radar approaches (5-10-4a)
items.back: 1 minute on vector to final approach, 15 seconds on surveillance final approach, 5 seconds on PAR final approach.

items.front: Lost comm pilot actions during radar approach (5-10-4a)
items.back: (1) Attempt contact on secondary/tower frequency. (2) Proceed VFR if possible. (3) Proceed with approved nonradar approach or execute specific lost comm procedure for the radar approach being used.

items.front: Radar contact lost before final approach starts (5-10-5)
items.back: Clear the aircraft to an appropriate NAVAID/fix for an instrument approach.

Advise pilot to PERFORM LANDING CHECK while on downwind leg and in time to complete before turning base. If incomplete pattern: before handoff for PAR, or before starting descent for surveillance approach.
Inform the aircraft of its position at least once before starting final approach. Phraseology: '(Number) MILES (direction) OF (airport) AIRPORT' or '...ON DOWNWIND/BASE LEG.'
Include the name of the facility. Phraseology: 'CONTACT (name of facility) FINAL CONTROLLER ON (frequency).'
On initial contact with final controller, ask for communication check. Phraseology: '(call sign), (facility) FINAL CONTROLLER. HOW DO YOU HEAR ME?'
After contact with final controller and while on final approach course, instruct aircraft: 'DO NOT ACKNOWLEDGE FURTHER TRANSMISSIONS.'

Before aircraft starts final descent for full-stop landing AND weather indicates any portion of final will be IFR: issue specific missed approach procedure. Phraseology: 'YOUR MISSED APPROACH PROCEDURE IS (procedure).'

Before final descent: issue departure instructions. Include specific heading and altitude except when maintaining VFR and contacting tower. Phraseology: 'AFTER COMPLETING LOW APPROACH/TOUCH AND GO: CLIMB AND MAINTAIN (altitude). TURN (right/left) HEADING (degrees)/FLY RUNWAY HEADING.' May be omitted for repeated approaches if unchanged.

(a) On final to tower-served airport: obtain clearance to land/touch-and-go/low approach, issue clearance and surface wind. (b) If clearance not obtained or canceled: inform aircraft and issue alternative instructions. Phraseology: 'TOWER CLEARANCE CANCELED/NOT RECEIVED (alternative instructions).'

items.front: Final approach abnormalities requiring missed approach (5-10-14)
items.back: Instruct aircraft if runway not in sight to execute missed approach (or climb/maintain altitude and fly specified course) when: (a) safety limits exceeded or radical target deviations, (b) position/ID in doubt, (c) radar contact lost or malfunction suspected, (d) airport conditions/traffic preclude completion. Subparagraphs a-c do NOT apply after PAR decision height.

items.front: Military single frequency approach - frequency change restrictions (5-10-15b)
items.back: Do NOT require frequency change after approach has begun unless: (1) landing/low approach completed, (2) VFR daylight, (3) pilot requests, (4) emergency, (5) cleared visual approach, (6) pilot cancels IFR. Controller handles comm transfer by positioning transmitter/receiver selectors - do NOT require flight to check on frequency.
